articles.headline: India Unveils Enhanced Incentives for Green Hydrogen Manufacturing Under National Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) announced a new phase of incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The enhanced scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
• Focus includes Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes for manufacturing and demand creation mechanisms.
• This move is critical for India to achieve its energy independence and decarbonization targets by 2047.
• The mission targets production of 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023 with an outlay of Rs 19,744 crore.
Target: 5 MMT green hydrogen production by 2030.
SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a major component.
Benefits: Decarbonization, reduced fossil fuel imports, indigenous manufacturing, job creation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the role of government incentives (PLI) in fostering a domestic green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and the potential solutions.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of a robust green hydrogen economy for India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the production cycle of green hydrogen from renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-emission fuel.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Amendments to Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a crucial verdict, largely affirming the constitutional validity of recent amendments to the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980.
• Petitioners had challenged the amendments, arguing they diluted environmental protections and bypassed parliamentary scrutiny.
• The court emphasized the need to balance development with conservation, noting the amendments aim to clarify the scope of the act and facilitate strategic projects.
• The ruling is expected to streamline clearances for certain infrastructure projects while mandating compensatory afforestation and strict environmental safeguards.
• Concerns remain regarding potential impacts on previously unprotected forest lands and tribal rights.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980 (FCA) is a central act for forest protection.
Amendments passed in 2023 aimed to broaden exemptions for certain land types and strategic projects.
The term 'forest land' and its interpretation has been a key point of contention.
Supreme Court's role in interpreting environmental laws and balancing competing interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on forest conservation and infrastructure development in India.
Discuss the challenges in defining 'forest land' and the impact of such definitions on tribal rights and local communities.
Critically evaluate the 'development vs. environment' debate in the context of India's growth trajectory and legal framework.
Suggest measures to strengthen environmental governance while ensuring sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A legal gavel resting on a stack of law books, with a forest background.
articles.trivia: The 'Godavarman judgment' (1996) significantly expanded the definition of 'forest' under the FCA to include any area recorded as forest in government records, irrespective of its ownership or classification.

articles.headline: India-EU Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Trade, Technology, and Green Transition
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-EU Strategic Dialogue concluded in Brussels, focusing on deepening cooperation across multiple sectors.
• Key areas of discussion included progress on the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations, digital cooperation, and climate action.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a rules-based international order and addressing global challenges like climate change and supply chain resilience.
• A roadmap for cooperation in critical and emerging technologies was also adopted.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence between India and the EU on geopolitical and geo-economic issues.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and EU are strategic partners since 2004.
EU is one of India's largest trading partners.
FTA negotiations resumed in 2022.
Key pillars of cooperation: Trade, Technology, Security, Climate.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-EU strategic partnership in the contemporary geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of the proposed India-EU Free Trade Agreement.
Examine how India and the EU can collaborate to address global challenges such as climate change, digital governance, and supply chain resilience.
Evaluate the role of technology cooperation in strengthening India-EU ties.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for €88 billion worth of trade in goods in 2021-22.

articles.headline: Nation Remembers Rani Lakshmibai on her Martyrdom Anniversary, Symbol of Valour in 1857 Uprising
articles.explainer:
• June 18 marks the martyrdom anniversary of Rani Lakshmibai, one of the most iconic figures of the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
• She famously led the resistance against the British in Jhansi and Gwalior, inspiring countless freedom fighters.
• Her unwavering courage and strategic prowess made her a formidable opponent, earning her the title 'Joan of Arc of India'.
• The day serves as a reminder of the sacrifices made during India's First War of Independence.
• Various commemorative events were held across the country to honour her legacy.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: Rani Lakshmibai (Manikarnika Tambe) was the Queen of the princely state of Jhansi.
Led the rebellion in Jhansi after the Doctrine of Lapse was applied.
Fought valiantly against Hugh Rose's forces.
Died fighting in Kotah-ki-Serai near Gwalior on June 18, 1858.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Rani Lakshmibai's role in the Indian Rebellion of 1857 and her enduring legacy.
Analyze the causes and nature of the 1857 Revolt, highlighting the contributions of various leaders.
Examine how the rebellion influenced subsequent nationalist movements in India.
Evaluate the impact of British policies like the Doctrine of Lapse on princely states and local rulers.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic depiction of Rani Lakshmibai on horseback, leading her troops in battle.
articles.trivia: General Hugh Rose, who commanded the British forces against Jhansi, remarked that 'among the mutineers, the best and bravest was the Rani of Jhansi.'

articles.headline: Renowned Naturalist and Wildlife Conservationist Maruti Chitampalli Passes Away at 92
articles.explainer:
• Maruti Chitampalli, a celebrated Indian naturalist, ornithologist, and author, passed away at the age of 92.
• Known for his extensive work in wildlife conservation, particularly in the Western Ghats and forests of Maharashtra.
• He served as a forest officer and contributed significantly to the establishment and management of several wildlife sanctuaries.
• Chitampalli's writings, blending scientific observation with literary flair, popularized wildlife among the masses.
• His legacy includes numerous books on birds, animals, and forest life, promoting ecological awareness.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: Maruti Chitampalli was a notable figure in Indian wildlife conservation.
Associated with the Western Ghats region, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and biodiversity hotspot.
His literary contributions helped popularize natural history in regional languages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of individuals like Maruti Chitampalli to wildlife conservation and environmental awareness in India.
Analyze the challenges facing biodiversity conservation in regions like the Western Ghats.
Examine the role of literature and popular science in fostering public engagement with environmental issues.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Maruti Chitampalli amidst a forest setting.
articles.trivia: Chitampalli was often referred to as 'Vanamitr' (friend of forests) for his deep connection and dedication to nature.

articles.headline: Legendary Mountaineer Captain Mohan Singh Kohli, First Indian Everest Expedition Leader, Passes Away at 93
articles.explainer:
• Captain Mohan Singh Kohli, a revered figure in Indian mountaineering, passed away at the age of 93.
• He led the historic 1965 Indian Everest Expedition, which saw the first successful ascent of Mount Everest by an Indian team.
• A decorated naval officer, Kohli was instrumental in promoting adventure sports and environmental conservation in the Himalayas.
• His leadership inspired a generation of mountaineers and significantly boosted India's standing in global mountaineering.
• He was a recipient of the Padma Bhushan and the Arjuna Award.
gsSubTopics: Important personalities.
Role of Sports in Nation Building.
prelimsPoints: Captain M.S. Kohli led the 1965 Indian Everest Expedition, the first successful Indian ascent.
He was a naval officer and a pioneer of adventure sports in India.
Recipient of Padma Bhushan and Arjuna Award.
His team put 9 climbers on Everest, a world record at the time.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of mountaineering expeditions in fostering national pride and international cooperation.
Examine the challenges and risks associated with high-altitude mountaineering and the role of leadership in such endeavors.
Analyze the contributions of individuals like M.S. Kohli to promoting adventure sports and fitness in India.
articles.imageDescription: A vintage photograph of Captain Mohan Singh Kohli in mountaineering gear, with Mount Everest in the background.
articles.trivia: The 1965 Indian Everest Expedition was so successful that it placed nine climbers on the summit, a record that stood for 17 years.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Decline in Wetland Ecosystems Across India
articles.explainer:
• A recent report released by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change reveals a significant decline in the health and extent of wetland ecosystems nationwide.
• The report attributes the degradation to urbanization, agricultural expansion, pollution, and climate change.
• Wetlands are crucial for biodiversity, water purification, flood control, and groundwater recharge.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for robust conservation strategies and stricter enforcement of environmental regulations.
• Emphasis was placed on community participation and integrated watershed management approaches.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Wetlands (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017 are in place for wetland protection.
India has designated many Ramsar Sites, which are wetlands of international importance.
Wetlands provide critical ecosystem services like carbon sequestration and supporting unique flora/fauna.
Threats: encroachment, pollution, invasive species, altered hydrology.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the factors contributing to their degradation in India.
Discuss the efficacy of existing policies and legal frameworks for wetland conservation.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including community-based approaches, for the sustainable management and restoration of wetland ecosystems.
Examine the linkage between wetland degradation and climate change impacts like increased flooding and drought.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a degraded wetland with signs of encroachment and pollution.
articles.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar Sites, covering a significant area, yet many non-Ramsar wetlands face severe threats.

articles.headline: ISRO Prepares for Launch of Advanced Communication Satellite for Enhanced Broadband Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the upcoming launch of its latest geostationary communication satellite.
• This satellite is designed to provide high-throughput broadband connectivity across India, including remote and rural areas.
• It will leverage advanced transponder technology to significantly boost internet speeds and data transmission capabilities.
• The mission is crucial for supporting India's digital initiatives, including e-governance, telemedicine, and online education.
• The launch is part of ISRO's ongoing efforts to strengthen India's satellite communication infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
Geostationary satellites orbit at an altitude of approximately 35,786 km above the Earth's equator.
Communication satellites play a vital role in DTH, VSAT, and broadband services.
The GSLV and LVM3 are ISRO's primary launch vehicles for geostationary missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of satellite communication for India's national development and strategic autonomy.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to 'Digital India' and social welfare schemes.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in enhancing India's satellite broadband infrastructure, especially for rural areas.
Evaluate India's position in the global space economy and the role of private sector participation.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's impression of a communication satellite orbiting Earth, with signals beaming down.
articles.trivia: India's first experimental communication satellite, APPLE (Ariane Passenger Payload Experiment), was launched by ISRO in 1981.

articles.headline: RBI Signals Continued Caution on Inflation Amidst Global Economic Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor reiterated a cautious stance on inflation, citing persistent global supply chain disruptions and volatile commodity prices.
• Despite recent moderation in some inflation indicators, the RBI emphasized maintaining monetary policy vigilance to anchor inflation expectations.
• The focus remains on achieving the medium-term inflation target while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued stability in interest rates in the short term, with an eye on global central bank actions.
• The RBI highlighted the need for fiscal policy support to manage inflationary pressures effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI's primary mandate is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy interest rate (repo rate).
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2% (2-6%).
Global factors like crude oil prices and geopolitical events significantly impact India's inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in a volatile global environment.
Discuss the tools and strategies employed by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to achieve its objectives.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in addressing India's economic challenges.
Evaluate the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's macroeconomic stability.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo superimposed on a graph showing economic indicators.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms in India's Disaster Management Framework
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending significant reforms to India's disaster management policies and infrastructure.
• Key recommendations include strengthening early warning systems, enhancing community-level preparedness, and improving inter-agency coordination.
• The report also called for greater investment in resilient infrastructure and a focus on climate change adaptation in disaster planning.
• It highlighted the need for a dedicated disaster relief fund at the state level and regular capacity building for first responders.
• The recommendations aim to shift focus from reactive relief to proactive risk reduction and mitigation.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Disaster Management Act, 2005 provides the legal framework for disaster management in India.
NDMA is the apex body for disaster management, headed by the Prime Minister.
SDMAs are chaired by Chief Ministers at the state level.
India is highly vulnerable to various natural disasters like floods, droughts, cyclones, and earthquakes.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate India's current disaster management framework, identifying its strengths and weaknesses.
Discuss the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee for reforming disaster management and their potential impact.
Examine the role of technology, community participation, and climate change adaptation in building disaster resilience.
Suggest measures to improve inter-agency coordination and resource mobilization for effective disaster response and mitigation.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers conducting operations during a flood, symbolizing disaster management efforts.
articles.trivia: The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030) is a global agreement to reduce disaster risk and losses.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive Reforms of the UN Security Council
articles.explainer:
• India once again emphasized the urgent need for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a multilateral forum.
• The Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson highlighted that the current composition of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary global realities.
• India advocates for expansion in both permanent and non-permanent categories, with a permanent seat for India.
• The reforms are crucial for enhancing the council's legitimacy, effectiveness, and representative character.
• India stressed that a reformed UNSC is essential to address evolving global challenges more effectively.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5: US, UK, France, Russia, China) with veto power.
It has 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
India has served multiple terms as a non-permanent member.
G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent seats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with a focus on India's aspirations.
Discuss the implications of the current UNSC structure for global peace and security.
Examine the challenges and prospects of achieving consensus on UNSC reforms in the present international political climate.
Evaluate India's growing role in global affairs and its claim for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
articles.imageDescription: The United Nations logo with flags of member states, symbolizing international diplomacy.
articles.trivia: The last major reform of the UN Security Council occurred in 1965 when the number of non-permanent members was increased from 6 to 10.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Indigenous Semiconductor Manufacturing Technology
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at a leading Indian institute announced a significant breakthrough in developing advanced indigenous technology for semiconductor manufacturing.
• The innovation focuses on novel materials and fabrication processes that could reduce dependence on foreign technology.
• This development is crucial for India's ambitions in becoming a global hub for electronics manufacturing and design.
• It aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities and boost domestic capabilities in strategic sectors.
• The government lauded the efforts as a step towards 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) in high-tech industries.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Semiconductors are essential components in almost all electronic devices.
India has launched the 'India Semiconductor Mission' to promote domestic chip manufacturing and design.
Global semiconductor supply chain issues highlighted the need for self-reliance.
Key challenges include high capital investment, skilled workforce, and advanced R&D.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous semiconductor manufacturing for India's economic growth and national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust semiconductor ecosystem in India.
Examine the role of government policies and R&D in fostering self-reliance in critical technologies.
Evaluate the potential impact of such breakthroughs on India's 'Digital India' and 'Make in India' initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of a silicon wafer with intricate circuit patterns, representing semiconductor technology.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach over a trillion dollars by the end of the decade.

articles.headline: National Education Policy: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development Intensifies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced further initiatives to bolster vocational training and skill development under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• New guidelines encourage schools and higher education institutions to integrate vocational courses from an early stage.
• Emphasis is on industry partnerships, apprenticeship programs, and skill-based learning to enhance employability.
• The move aims to address the skill gap in the Indian workforce and align education with market demands.
• A national framework for credit accumulation and transfer for vocational courses is also being finalized.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry - the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education.
Key focus areas include early childhood care, foundational literacy, and vocational integration.
Skill India Mission is a government initiative to train 40 crore people in various skills by 2022 (ongoing).
Vocational education is proposed to be integrated into mainstream education from Class 6 onwards.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the increased focus on vocational training and skill development under NEP 2020.
Discuss the potential benefits of integrating vocational education into mainstream curricula for enhancing employability and addressing the skill gap.
Examine the challenges in implementing vocational training programs effectively across diverse educational institutions.
Suggest measures to strengthen industry-academia linkages and ensure the relevance of vocational skills to market demands.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on vocational training, such as carpentry or electronics.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: Tributes Paid to Veteran Screenwriter Prafulla Roy, Architect of Bengali Cinema's Golden Era
articles.explainer:
• Prafulla Roy, a celebrated Bengali screenwriter and novelist, passed away at the age of 91, leaving behind a rich cinematic legacy.
• He was instrumental in shaping the narrative landscape of Bengali cinema, contributing to numerous critically acclaimed films.
• Roy's screenplays were known for their nuanced characters, social realism, and profound storytelling.
• His work often explored the complexities of human relationships and societal issues, resonating deeply with audiences.
• He collaborated with many legendary directors and actors, making an indelible mark on Indian film history.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important personalities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of regional cinema, specifically Bengali cinema, to the evolution of Indian filmmaking.
Examine how screenwriters like Prafulla Roy used their craft to reflect and influence societal narratives.
Analyze the role of art and culture in shaping public discourse and preserving cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Prafulla Roy, perhaps with a film reel in the background.
articles.trivia: Bengali cinema is often credited with pioneering the 'Parallel Cinema' movement in India, emphasizing realism and artistic merit over commercial appeal.

articles.headline: Anthropologist and Academic Administrator Pooran Chand Joshi Passes Away at 69
articles.explainer:
• Pooran Chand Joshi, a distinguished anthropologist and former academic administrator, passed away at the age of 69.
• He was known for his extensive research on tribal communities and rural development in India.
• Joshi served in various capacities, contributing to policy formulation and academic institution building.
• His work emphasized the importance of indigenous knowledge systems and sustainable development practices.
• He leaves behind a legacy of scholarship that informed social policy and promoted a deeper understanding of Indian society.
gsSubTopics: Indian Society: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Important personalities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of anthropology and social sciences in understanding and addressing developmental challenges in India.
Examine the significance of indigenous knowledge systems in promoting sustainable development, particularly for tribal communities.
Analyze the contributions of academics and researchers in shaping social policies and fostering inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A formal portrait of Pooran Chand Joshi.
articles.trivia: Anthropology in India has a rich history, deeply intertwined with colonial administration and later with post-independence nation-building efforts.

articles.headline: Former Indian Cricketer Dilip Doshi Passes Away at 77
articles.explainer:
• Dilip Doshi, a former left-arm spinner who represented India in Test cricket, passed away at the age of 77.
• Doshi was known for his subtle variations and control, making a significant impact in his relatively short international career.
• He made his Test debut at a late age but quickly established himself as a reliable performer.
• His career spanned both domestic and international cricket, earning him respect for his dedication and skill.
• Tributes poured in from the cricketing fraternity, remembering his contribution to Indian cricket.
gsSubTopics: Important personalities.
Role of Sports in Nation Building.
articles.imageDescription: A vintage photograph of Dilip Doshi bowling during a cricket match.
articles.trivia: Dilip Doshi is one of the few cricketers to have made his Test debut after the age of 30 and still picked up over 100 Test wickets.

articles.headline: Veteran Marathi Stage Actor Vivek Lagoo Passes Away at 71
articles.explainer:
• Vivek Lagoo, a respected veteran of Marathi theatre and film, passed away at 71.
• Known for his versatile performances and significant contributions to Marathi stage for decades.
• He was a prominent figure in the Marathi cultural scene, acclaimed for both comedic and dramatic roles.
• His demise marks a loss for the regional theatre community, where he was a mentor to many.
• Tributes highlighted his dedication to the performing arts and his indelible impact on regional culture.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important personalities.
articles.imageDescription: A photograph of Vivek Lagoo on stage, performing.
articles.trivia: Marathi theatre has a rich history dating back to the mid-19th century, often blending traditional folk forms with Western dramatic structures.

articles.headline: Young Actor and Director Tushar Ghadigaonkar Passes Away at 32
articles.explainer:
• Tushar Ghadigaonkar, a promising young actor and director in the regional film industry, passed away at the age of 32.
• His untimely demise has saddened the regional entertainment fraternity.
• Ghadigaonkar was known for his innovative approach to storytelling and his contributions to independent cinema.
• His work often explored contemporary themes and gave a voice to emerging talent.
• He was considered a rising star with a bright future in the industry.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important personalities.
articles.trivia: The regional film industries in India collectively produce more films annually than Bollywood, catering to diverse linguistic and cultural audiences.

highlights: India announces enhanced incentives for Green Hydrogen manufacturing, boosting energy independence and decarbonization goals.
Supreme Court upholds amendments to the Forest (Conservation) Act, balancing development with environmental safeguards.
India and EU conclude strategic dialogue, emphasizing cooperation on trade, technology, and climate action.
Nation commemorates the martyrdom anniversary of Rani Lakshmibai, a pivotal figure of the 1857 Indian Rebellion.
ISRO prepares for the launch of an advanced communication satellite to enhance broadband connectivity across India.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary objective is to achieve 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen production annually by 2030.
2. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme is a key component of the mission.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 MMT production by 2030, includes the SIGHT Programme for incentives, and green hydrogen is indeed produced via electrolysis using renewable energy sources.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the significance of Rani Lakshmibai in Indian history?
options: (a) She was the first woman ruler of the Mughal Empire.
(b) She played a pivotal role in the Indian Rebellion of 1857, leading resistance in Jhansi and Gwalior.
(c) She was the founder of the Maratha Empire and a great warrior.
(d) She led the Salt Satyagraha movement against British rule.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) She played a pivotal role in the Indian Rebellion of 1857, leading resistance in Jhansi and Gwalior.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai was the Queen of Jhansi and a prominent leader of the 1857 Rebellion, fighting bravely against the British. Options (a), (c), and (d) refer to different historical figures or periods.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980:
1. It is a central act for the conservation of forests in India.
2. The Supreme Court's 'Godavarman judgment' (1996) broadened the definition of 'forest' under this act.
3. Recent amendments to the act have been challenged for potentially diluting environmental protections.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The FCA 1980 is a central act. The Godavarman judgment expanded the definition of forest. Recent amendments have indeed faced legal challenges regarding their impact on forest protection.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following international organizations has 5 permanent members with veto power and 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The United Nations Security Council (UNSC) is composed of five permanent members (P5) with veto power and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms. The other options do not fit this description.

prelimsMocks.question: Captain Mohan Singh Kohli is primarily known for his contributions to which field?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Captain Mohan Singh Kohli was a legendary Indian mountaineer who led the 1965 Indian Everest Expedition, the first successful Indian ascent of Mount Everest.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze India's National Green Hydrogen Mission. Discuss its potential to transform India's energy landscape and the key challenges in its successful implementation. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its strategic importance for energy security, decarbonization, and economic growth. Analyze the opportunities it presents (job creation, export potential, reduced imports). Detail key challenges like high capital costs, technology scaling, infrastructure development, and policy support. Conclude with suggestions for effective implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent decision on the Forest (Conservation) Act amendments highlights the perennial debate between development and environmental protection. Examine this conflict in the Indian context and suggest a balanced approach for sustainable development. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions; GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the Forest (Conservation) Act and the nature of the recent amendments and the Supreme Court's ruling. Discuss the 'development vs. environment' conflict with examples. Analyze the arguments for both sides (economic growth, infrastructure vs. ecological fragility, tribal rights). Propose a balanced approach incorporating principles of sustainable development, EIA, compensatory afforestation, community participation, and green technologies.

mainsMocks.question: Rani Lakshmibai remains an enduring symbol of courage and resistance in Indian history. Discuss her role in the Indian Rebellion of 1857 and analyze how the rebellion contributed to the subsequent nationalist movements in India. (10 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues; The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Rani Lakshmibai and her significant contribution to the 1857 Revolt, detailing her leadership in Jhansi and Gwalior. Briefly touch upon the causes of the revolt. Analyze how the 1857 rebellion, despite its failure, sowed the seeds of nationalism, unified diverse sections against British rule, and provided a heroic narrative for future freedom fighters. Conclude with its long-term impact on the Indian independence movement.

quickBytes.text: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production.
Rani Lakshmibai, the Queen of Jhansi, was martyred on June 18, 1858, during the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
The Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980, is a key legislation for protecting forest lands in India.
India is a member of the G4 nations, advocating for permanent seats in the UN Security Council.
Captain Mohan Singh Kohli led the 1965 Indian Everest Expedition, the first successful Indian ascent of Mount Everest.
ISRO's geostationary communication satellites are vital for India's DTH, VSAT, and broadband services.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets India's policy interest rate, primarily focusing on inflation targeting.
Wetlands are crucial ecosystems providing services like water purification and flood control, facing significant threats.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 emphasizes integrating vocational training from early stages.
India Semiconductor Mission aims to boost domestic chip manufacturing and design, fostering 'Atmanirbhar Bharat'.

GS1.headline: Nation Remembers Rani Lakshmibai on her Martyrdom Anniversary, Symbol of Valour in 1857 Uprising
GS1.explainer:
• June 18 marks the martyrdom anniversary of Rani Lakshmibai, one of the most iconic figures of the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
• She famously led the resistance against the British in Jhansi and Gwalior, inspiring countless freedom fighters.
• Her unwavering courage and strategic prowess made her a formidable opponent, earning her the title 'Joan of Arc of India'.
• The day serves as a reminder of the sacrifices made during India's First War of Independence.
• Various commemorative events were held across the country to honour her legacy.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues.
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
prelimsPoints: Rani Lakshmibai (Manikarnika Tambe) was the Queen of the princely state of Jhansi.
Led the rebellion in Jhansi after the Doctrine of Lapse was applied.
Fought valiantly against Hugh Rose's forces.
Died fighting in Kotah-ki-Serai near Gwalior on June 18, 1858.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Rani Lakshmibai's role in the Indian Rebellion of 1857 and her enduring legacy.
Analyze the causes and nature of the 1857 Revolt, highlighting the contributions of various leaders.
Examine how the rebellion influenced subsequent nationalist movements in India.
Evaluate the impact of British policies like the Doctrine of Lapse on princely states and local rulers.
GS1.imageDescription: An artistic depiction of Rani Lakshmibai on horseback, leading her troops in battle.
GS1.trivia: General Hugh Rose, who commanded the British forces against Jhansi, remarked that 'among the mutineers, the best and bravest was the Rani of Jhansi.'

GS1.headline: Legendary Mountaineer Captain Mohan Singh Kohli, First Indian Everest Expedition Leader, Passes Away at 93
GS1.explainer:
• Captain Mohan Singh Kohli, a revered figure in Indian mountaineering, passed away at the age of 93.
• He led the historic 1965 Indian Everest Expedition, which saw the first successful ascent of Mount Everest by an Indian team.
• A decorated naval officer, Kohli was instrumental in promoting adventure sports and environmental conservation in the Himalayas.
• His leadership inspired a generation of mountaineers and significantly boosted India's standing in global mountaineering.
• He was a recipient of the Padma Bhushan and the Arjuna Award.
gsSubTopics: Important personalities.
Role of Sports in Nation Building.
prelimsPoints: Captain M.S. Kohli led the 1965 Indian Everest Expedition, the first successful Indian ascent.
He was a naval officer and a pioneer of adventure sports in India.
Recipient of Padma Bhushan and Arjuna Award.
His team put 9 climbers on Everest, a world record at the time.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of mountaineering expeditions in fostering national pride and international cooperation.
Examine the challenges and risks associated with high-altitude mountaineering and the role of leadership in such endeavors.
Analyze the contributions of individuals like M.S. Kohli to promoting adventure sports and fitness in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A vintage photograph of Captain Mohan Singh Kohli in mountaineering gear, with Mount Everest in the background.
GS1.trivia: The 1965 Indian Everest Expedition was so successful that it placed nine climbers on the summit, a record that stood for 17 years.

GS1.headline: Tributes Paid to Veteran Screenwriter Prafulla Roy, Architect of Bengali Cinema's Golden Era
GS1.explainer:
• Prafulla Roy, a celebrated Bengali screenwriter and novelist, passed away at the age of 91, leaving behind a rich cinematic legacy.
• He was instrumental in shaping the narrative landscape of Bengali cinema, contributing to numerous critically acclaimed films.
• Roy's screenplays were known for their nuanced characters, social realism, and profound storytelling.
• His work often explored the complexities of human relationships and societal issues, resonating deeply with audiences.
• He collaborated with many legendary directors and actors, making an indelible mark on Indian film history.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important personalities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of regional cinema, specifically Bengali cinema, to the evolution of Indian filmmaking.
Examine how screenwriters like Prafulla Roy used their craft to reflect and influence societal narratives.
Analyze the role of art and culture in shaping public discourse and preserving cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of Prafulla Roy, perhaps with a film reel in the background.
GS1.trivia: Bengali cinema is often credited with pioneering the 'Parallel Cinema' movement in India, emphasizing realism and artistic merit over commercial appeal.

GS1.headline: Anthropologist and Academic Administrator Pooran Chand Joshi Passes Away at 69
GS1.explainer:
• Pooran Chand Joshi, a distinguished anthropologist and former academic administrator, passed away at the age of 69.
• He was known for his extensive research on tribal communities and rural development in India.
• Joshi served in various capacities, contributing to policy formulation and academic institution building.
• His work emphasized the importance of indigenous knowledge systems and sustainable development practices.
• He leaves behind a legacy of scholarship that informed social policy and promoted a deeper understanding of Indian society.
gsSubTopics: Indian Society: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
Important personalities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of anthropology and social sciences in understanding and addressing developmental challenges in India.
Examine the significance of indigenous knowledge systems in promoting sustainable development, particularly for tribal communities.
Analyze the contributions of academics and researchers in shaping social policies and fostering inclusive growth.
GS1.imageDescription: A formal portrait of Pooran Chand Joshi.
GS1.trivia: Anthropology in India has a rich history, deeply intertwined with colonial administration and later with post-independence nation-building efforts.

GS1.headline: Former Indian Cricketer Dilip Doshi Passes Away at 77
GS1.explainer:
• Dilip Doshi, a former left-arm spinner who represented India in Test cricket, passed away at the age of 77.
• Doshi was known for his subtle variations and control, making a significant impact in his relatively short international career.
• He made his Test debut at a late age but quickly established himself as a reliable performer.
• His career spanned both domestic and international cricket, earning him respect for his dedication and skill.
• Tributes poured in from the cricketing fraternity, remembering his contribution to Indian cricket.
gsSubTopics: Important personalities.
Role of Sports in Nation Building.
GS1.imageDescription: A vintage photograph of Dilip Doshi bowling during a cricket match.
GS1.trivia: Dilip Doshi is one of the few cricketers to have made his Test debut after the age of 30 and still picked up over 100 Test wickets.

GS1.headline: Veteran Marathi Stage Actor Vivek Lagoo Passes Away at 71
GS1.explainer:
• Vivek Lagoo, a respected veteran of Marathi theatre and film, passed away at 71.
• Known for his versatile performances and significant contributions to Marathi stage for decades.
• He was a prominent figure in the Marathi cultural scene, acclaimed for both comedic and dramatic roles.
• His demise marks a loss for the regional theatre community, where he was a mentor to many.
• Tributes highlighted his dedication to the performing arts and his indelible impact on regional culture.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important personalities.
GS1.imageDescription: A photograph of Vivek Lagoo on stage, performing.
GS1.trivia: Marathi theatre has a rich history dating back to the mid-19th century, often blending traditional folk forms with Western dramatic structures.

GS1.headline: Young Actor and Director Tushar Ghadigaonkar Passes Away at 32
GS1.explainer:
• Tushar Ghadigaonkar, a promising young actor and director in the regional film industry, passed away at the age of 32.
• His untimely demise has saddened the regional entertainment fraternity.
• Ghadigaonkar was known for his innovative approach to storytelling and his contributions to independent cinema.
• His work often explored contemporary themes and gave a voice to emerging talent.
• He was considered a rising star with a bright future in the industry.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Important personalities.
GS1.trivia: The regional film industries in India collectively produce more films annually than Bollywood, catering to diverse linguistic and cultural audiences.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Amendments to Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India delivered a crucial verdict, largely affirming the constitutional validity of recent amendments to the Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980.
• Petitioners had challenged the amendments, arguing they diluted environmental protections and bypassed parliamentary scrutiny.
• The court emphasized the need to balance development with conservation, noting the amendments aim to clarify the scope of the act and facilitate strategic projects.
• The ruling is expected to streamline clearances for certain infrastructure projects while mandating compensatory afforestation and strict environmental safeguards.
• Concerns remain regarding potential impacts on previously unprotected forest lands and tribal rights.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Forest (Conservation) Act, 1980 (FCA) is a central act for forest protection.
Amendments passed in 2023 aimed to broaden exemptions for certain land types and strategic projects.
The term 'forest land' and its interpretation has been a key point of contention.
Supreme Court's role in interpreting environmental laws and balancing competing interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on forest conservation and infrastructure development in India.
Discuss the challenges in defining 'forest land' and the impact of such definitions on tribal rights and local communities.
Critically evaluate the 'development vs. environment' debate in the context of India's growth trajectory and legal framework.
Suggest measures to strengthen environmental governance while ensuring sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: A legal gavel resting on a stack of law books, with a forest background.
GS2.trivia: The 'Godavarman judgment' (1996) significantly expanded the definition of 'forest' under the FCA to include any area recorded as forest in government records, irrespective of its ownership or classification.

GS2.headline: India-EU Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizing Trade, Technology, and Green Transition
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-EU Strategic Dialogue concluded in Brussels, focusing on deepening cooperation across multiple sectors.
• Key areas of discussion included progress on the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations, digital cooperation, and climate action.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to a rules-based international order and addressing global challenges like climate change and supply chain resilience.
• A roadmap for cooperation in critical and emerging technologies was also adopted.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence between India and the EU on geopolitical and geo-economic issues.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and EU are strategic partners since 2004.
EU is one of India's largest trading partners.
FTA negotiations resumed in 2022.
Key pillars of cooperation: Trade, Technology, Security, Climate.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-EU strategic partnership in the contemporary geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges of the proposed India-EU Free Trade Agreement.
Examine how India and the EU can collaborate to address global challenges such as climate change, digital governance, and supply chain resilience.
Evaluate the role of technology cooperation in strengthening India-EU ties.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed side-by-side during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: The EU is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for €88 billion worth of trade in goods in 2021-22.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Comprehensive Reforms of the UN Security Council
GS2.explainer:
• India once again emphasized the urgent need for comprehensive reforms of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) at a multilateral forum.
• The Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson highlighted that the current composition of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary global realities.
• India advocates for expansion in both permanent and non-permanent categories, with a permanent seat for India.
• The reforms are crucial for enhancing the council's legitimacy, effectiveness, and representative character.
• India stressed that a reformed UNSC is essential to address evolving global challenges more effectively.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: UNSC has 5 permanent members (P5: US, UK, France, Russia, China) with veto power.
It has 10 non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.
India has served multiple terms as a non-permanent member.
G4 nations (India, Brazil, Germany, Japan) advocate for permanent seats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, with a focus on India's aspirations.
Discuss the implications of the current UNSC structure for global peace and security.
Examine the challenges and prospects of achieving consensus on UNSC reforms in the present international political climate.
Evaluate India's growing role in global affairs and its claim for a permanent seat in the UNSC.
GS2.imageDescription: The United Nations logo with flags of member states, symbolizing international diplomacy.
GS2.trivia: The last major reform of the UN Security Council occurred in 1965 when the number of non-permanent members was increased from 6 to 10.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development Intensifies
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced further initiatives to bolster vocational training and skill development under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• New guidelines encourage schools and higher education institutions to integrate vocational courses from an early stage.
• Emphasis is on industry partnerships, apprenticeship programs, and skill-based learning to enhance employability.
• The move aims to address the skill gap in the Indian workforce and align education with market demands.
• A national framework for credit accumulation and transfer for vocational courses is also being finalized.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry - the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education.
Key focus areas include early childhood care, foundational literacy, and vocational integration.
Skill India Mission is a government initiative to train 40 crore people in various skills by 2022 (ongoing).
Vocational education is proposed to be integrated into mainstream education from Class 6 onwards.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the increased focus on vocational training and skill development under NEP 2020.
Discuss the potential benefits of integrating vocational education into mainstream curricula for enhancing employability and addressing the skill gap.
Examine the challenges in implementing vocational training programs effectively across diverse educational institutions.
Suggest measures to strengthen industry-academia linkages and ensure the relevance of vocational skills to market demands.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on vocational training, such as carpentry or electronics.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those in 1968 and 1986.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Enhanced Incentives for Green Hydrogen Manufacturing Under National Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) announced a new phase of incentives under the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The enhanced scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
• Focus includes Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes for manufacturing and demand creation mechanisms.
• This move is critical for India to achieve its energy independence and decarbonization targets by 2047.
• The mission targets production of 5 million metric tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched in 2023 with an outlay of Rs 19,744 crore.
Target: 5 MMT green hydrogen production by 2030.
SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a major component.
Benefits: Decarbonization, reduced fossil fuel imports, indigenous manufacturing, job creation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the role of government incentives (PLI) in fostering a domestic green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and the potential solutions.
Evaluate the economic and environmental benefits of a robust green hydrogen economy for India.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the production cycle of green hydrogen from renewable energy sources.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-emission fuel.

GS3.headline: Renowned Naturalist and Wildlife Conservationist Maruti Chitampalli Passes Away at 92
GS3.explainer:
• Maruti Chitampalli, a celebrated Indian naturalist, ornithologist, and author, passed away at the age of 92.
• Known for his extensive work in wildlife conservation, particularly in the Western Ghats and forests of Maharashtra.
• He served as a forest officer and contributed significantly to the establishment and management of several wildlife sanctuaries.
• Chitampalli's writings, blending scientific observation with literary flair, popularized wildlife among the masses.
• His legacy includes numerous books on birds, animals, and forest life, promoting ecological awareness.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
prelimsPoints: Maruti Chitampalli was a notable figure in Indian wildlife conservation.
Associated with the Western Ghats region, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and biodiversity hotspot.
His literary contributions helped popularize natural history in regional languages.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of individuals like Maruti Chitampalli to wildlife conservation and environmental awareness in India.
Analyze the challenges facing biodiversity conservation in regions like the Western Ghats.
Examine the role of literature and popular science in fostering public engagement with environmental issues.
GS3.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Maruti Chitampalli amidst a forest setting.
GS3.trivia: Chitampalli was often referred to as 'Vanamitr' (friend of forests) for his deep connection and dedication to nature.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Decline in Wetland Ecosystems Across India
GS3.explainer:
• A recent report released by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change reveals a significant decline in the health and extent of wetland ecosystems nationwide.
• The report attributes the degradation to urbanization, agricultural expansion, pollution, and climate change.
• Wetlands are crucial for biodiversity, water purification, flood control, and groundwater recharge.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for robust conservation strategies and stricter enforcement of environmental regulations.
• Emphasis was placed on community participation and integrated watershed management approaches.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Wetlands (Conservation and Management) Rules, 2017 are in place for wetland protection.
India has designated many Ramsar Sites, which are wetlands of international importance.
Wetlands provide critical ecosystem services like carbon sequestration and supporting unique flora/fauna.
Threats: encroachment, pollution, invasive species, altered hydrology.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological and economic significance of wetlands and the factors contributing to their degradation in India.
Discuss the efficacy of existing policies and legal frameworks for wetland conservation.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including community-based approaches, for the sustainable management and restoration of wetland ecosystems.
Examine the linkage between wetland degradation and climate change impacts like increased flooding and drought.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a degraded wetland with signs of encroachment and pollution.
GS3.trivia: India has over 75 Ramsar Sites, covering a significant area, yet many non-Ramsar wetlands face severe threats.

GS3.headline: ISRO Prepares for Launch of Advanced Communication Satellite for Enhanced Broadband Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the upcoming launch of its latest geostationary communication satellite.
• This satellite is designed to provide high-throughput broadband connectivity across India, including remote and rural areas.
• It will leverage advanced transponder technology to significantly boost internet speeds and data transmission capabilities.
• The mission is crucial for supporting India's digital initiatives, including e-governance, telemedicine, and online education.
• The launch is part of ISRO's ongoing efforts to strengthen India's satellite communication infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
Geostationary satellites orbit at an altitude of approximately 35,786 km above the Earth's equator.
Communication satellites play a vital role in DTH, VSAT, and broadband services.
The GSLV and LVM3 are ISRO's primary launch vehicles for geostationary missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of satellite communication for India's national development and strategic autonomy.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to 'Digital India' and social welfare schemes.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in enhancing India's satellite broadband infrastructure, especially for rural areas.
Evaluate India's position in the global space economy and the role of private sector participation.
GS3.imageDescription: An artist's impression of a communication satellite orbiting Earth, with signals beaming down.
GS3.trivia: India's first experimental communication satellite, APPLE (Ariane Passenger Payload Experiment), was launched by ISRO in 1981.

GS3.headline: RBI Signals Continued Caution on Inflation Amidst Global Economic Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor reiterated a cautious stance on inflation, citing persistent global supply chain disruptions and volatile commodity prices.
• Despite recent moderation in some inflation indicators, the RBI emphasized maintaining monetary policy vigilance to anchor inflation expectations.
• The focus remains on achieving the medium-term inflation target while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate continued stability in interest rates in the short term, with an eye on global central bank actions.
• The RBI highlighted the need for fiscal policy support to manage inflationary pressures effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI's primary mandate is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy interest rate (repo rate).
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2% (2-6%).
Global factors like crude oil prices and geopolitical events significantly impact India's inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in a volatile global environment.
Discuss the tools and strategies employed by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to achieve its objectives.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in addressing India's economic challenges.
Evaluate the impact of global economic uncertainties on India's macroeconomic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo superimposed on a graph showing economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.

GS3.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Sweeping Reforms in India's Disaster Management Framework
GS3.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted a report recommending significant reforms to India's disaster management policies and infrastructure.
• Key recommendations include strengthening early warning systems, enhancing community-level preparedness, and improving inter-agency coordination.
• The report also called for greater investment in resilient infrastructure and a focus on climate change adaptation in disaster planning.
• It highlighted the need for a dedicated disaster relief fund at the state level and regular capacity building for first responders.
• The recommendations aim to shift focus from reactive relief to proactive risk reduction and mitigation.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Disaster Management Act, 2005 provides the legal framework for disaster management in India.
NDMA is the apex body for disaster management, headed by the Prime Minister.
SDMAs are chaired by Chief Ministers at the state level.
India is highly vulnerable to various natural disasters like floods, droughts, cyclones, and earthquakes.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate India's current disaster management framework, identifying its strengths and weaknesses.
Discuss the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee for reforming disaster management and their potential impact.
Examine the role of technology, community participation, and climate change adaptation in building disaster resilience.
Suggest measures to improve inter-agency coordination and resource mobilization for effective disaster response and mitigation.
GS3.imageDescription: Rescue workers conducting operations during a flood, symbolizing disaster management efforts.
GS3.trivia: The Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030) is a global agreement to reduce disaster risk and losses.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Indigenous Semiconductor Manufacturing Technology
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at a leading Indian institute announced a significant breakthrough in developing advanced indigenous technology for semiconductor manufacturing.
• The innovation focuses on novel materials and fabrication processes that could reduce dependence on foreign technology.
• This development is crucial for India's ambitions in becoming a global hub for electronics manufacturing and design.
• It aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities and boost domestic capabilities in strategic sectors.
• The government lauded the efforts as a step towards 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) in high-tech industries.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Semiconductors are essential components in almost all electronic devices.
India has launched the 'India Semiconductor Mission' to promote domestic chip manufacturing and design.
Global semiconductor supply chain issues highlighted the need for self-reliance.
Key challenges include high capital investment, skilled workforce, and advanced R&D.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of indigenous semiconductor manufacturing for India's economic growth and national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in establishing a robust semiconductor ecosystem in India.
Examine the role of government policies and R&D in fostering self-reliance in critical technologies.
Evaluate the potential impact of such breakthroughs on India's 'Digital India' and 'Make in India' initiatives.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up of a silicon wafer with intricate circuit patterns, representing semiconductor technology.
GS3.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is projected to reach over a trillion dollars by the end of the decade.

sources: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
